title: feat(ibg-repository): canonical repository proposal pipeline contract (IBG-1 PR D)
intent: Introduce the canonical, orchestration-only RepositoryProposalPipeline that composes the existing (already-merged) proposal contracts into one deterministic entry point: build_proposal_target_binding (PR A) -> build_repository_change_proposal (PR A) -> build_review_bundle (PR C), terminating at the human-review boundary. The pipeline is deliberately thin: it owns no new validation, no new serialization, and no new authority; it only fixes the canonical composition order and the explicit terminal marker. It reuses existing contracts, preserves provenance, never upgrades authority, and performs no git/filesystem/GitHub/network operation. Ships with the owner-ratified contract document and invariant tests that bind these properties. This is a contract-definition sprint, not a feature layer; DraftPullRequestPackage and RepositoryReviewBundle (PR C) are reused, never re-created.
behavior_change: Adds one additive orchestration module (repository_proposal_pipeline.py) plus its invariant tests under the existing services/api/app/ibg_repository/ package. No existing runtime path imports it; no existing behavior, schema, authority, or governance changes. The module composes existing builders only — no git, no network, no filesystem, no GitHub, no authority mutation. Review hardening applied before merge: (1) target_branch is a REQUIRED argument on both run() and run_repository_proposal_pipeline() (no silent 'main' default) so the canonical entry point cannot emit wrong draft-PR base metadata; (2) the unused RepositoryProposalPipelineError type is removed from the module and the package export surface — the ratified contract is that each builder's own fail-closed error propagates UNWRAPPED, so a bespoke pipeline error would misrepresent the contract. This only trims a never-released additive export (PR D is not yet merged); no other public API changes.

diff_articulation.what_changed: Creates repository_proposal_pipeline.py: RepositoryProposalPipeline (a frozen, stateless orchestrator with a single run() operation), RepositoryProposalPipelineResult (frozen; carries the proposal + review bundle, a terminal human-review marker, and a to_canonical_dict that embeds the bundle's canonical form without duplicating the proposal), the ratified CANONICAL_PIPELINE_MISSION constant, and a run_repository_proposal_pipeline convenience. run() composes build_proposal_target_binding -> build_repository_change_proposal -> build_review_bundle and returns at the human-review boundary; it adds no logic and lets each builder's fail-closed errors propagate. __init__ additively re-exports the pipeline API. Invariant tests bind: reuses existing contracts (pipeline result equals direct composition; bundle embeds the proposal's own canonical dict), preserves provenance (reference always; verified lineage flows through; mismatch propagates), does not upgrade authority (candidate unmutated; state carried not promoted), terminates at human review (only run() is public; no execute/commit/push/PR verb; result frozen), deterministic output, and an AST-precise invariant that the module imports no subprocess/requests/httpx/urllib/socket/http/os/pygithub and does not import PR-B git_runner. The owner-ratified contract doc is added under docs/architecture. REVIEW HARDENING (pre-merge): target_branch is now a required argument (no 'main' default) on run() and the convenience wrapper; the unused RepositoryProposalPipelineError type is removed from the module and package exports (builder errors propagate unwrapped, per the contract); the broad pytest.raises(Exception) guards are tightened to the specific builder error types (EvidenceContractError, RepositoryChangeProposalError, RepositoryReviewBundleError, FrozenInstanceError); and integration tests are added for malformed-but-not-None review_package/workspace_metadata (fail-closed) and for target_branch being required (TypeError when omitted).
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: No canonical composition entry point existed; PR A/B/C shipped the individual contracts but nothing fixed the canonical order or the explicit terminal boundary, and nothing bound the cross-contract invariants (provenance preserved end-to-end, authority never upgraded, no execution) in one place. The pipeline composes and does not duplicate: DraftPullRequestPackage and RepositoryReviewBundle are reused via build_review_bundle, not re-created. This is the ratified architectural spine that future IBG repository work must obey.

commands_run: python -m pytest tests/ibg_repository/ -q  ->  all passed (PR A + PR B + PR C + PR D together)

py -3.11 -m pytest tests/ibg_repository/test_repository_proposal_pipeline.py --no-cov -q  ->  19 passed (15 baseline + 4 review-hardening: malformed review_package/workspace_metadata, target_branch-required, tightened exception types)

files.intent: Additively re-export the PR D pipeline API.
Thin canonical orchestrator composing existing builders; terminates at human review.
Invariant tests binding the ratified mission.
Per-PR CBSP21 manifest for PR D.

architecture_scan.acknowledgement_notes: Additive, orchestration-only. Composes existing merged contracts (PR A/B/C); no logic/contract duplication. No git/filesystem/GitHub/network/authority. Terminates at the human-review boundary. Execution planning (PR E, descriptive) and any execution engine (PR F) are out of scope.
